claim: Evaluation must be defined numerically for business success before writing any code or selecting models
actionable: Define success metrics with concrete numbers (e.g., 60% deflection rate, 85% accuracy) before model selection
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Three-layer evaluation architecture reduces cost: deterministic (regex/ML) → semantic (LLM judges) → behavioral (tool call validation)
actionable: Implement deterministic layer first, add LLM judges for groundedness/safety, then validate tool call sequences and API efficiency
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Tracing every agent decision is mandatory for regulatory compliance, not optional for production AI systems
actionable: Implement comprehensive tracing infrastructure before deployment; European regulators mandate observability for AI to be production-ready
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Model selection should happen in week 7-8 of an 8-week engagement, after evaluation and infrastructure setup
actionable: Spend first 6 weeks on evaluation pipeline, data foundation, and tracing; only then compare models on your evaluation dataset to pick winners
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Evaluation dataset must be a living system that grows continuously in production, not a static benchmark
actionable: Start with 200 test cases; systematically add new cases when production issues occur or edge cases are discovered; use automated evaluation pipeline to run new test cases continuously
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Data quality is the dominant bottleneck in agent deployment: agents do not forgive bad data like humans do
actionable: Audit all data sources for accuracy before agents access them; implement deterministic validation and entity recognition to catch bad data before agents use it
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Tracing system caught outdated embeddings causing agent failures: when policy documents change, re-embed immediately or agent serves stale answers
actionable: Set up alerts when source documents are updated; automatically re-embed documents and purge old embeddings from vector DB to prevent stale answers
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Behavioral evaluation layer catches expensive inefficiencies: validate that agents do not make duplicate API calls or enter retry loops
actionable: Monitor tool call sequences in production; set limits on retry attempts (e.g., max 3 retries); alert and fallback when duplicate calls detected
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: PII detection and governance flagged 47 PII breaches during testing before production launch
actionable: Implement PII detection using NER and entity recognition before agents access data; apply this validation layer early in testing, not in production
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Prompt versioning must follow change management processes like code, with commit messages explaining why changes were made
actionable: Treat prompts as code; require detailed commit messages documenting what failure was fixed and what problem the new prompt version solves
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Multi-agent orchestration requires distinct patterns: orchestrator-worker (centralized control), choreography (independent agents on message bus), human-in-loop (threshold-based)
actionable: Choose orchestrator-worker for sequential dependencies; choreography for parallel work; human-in-loop when confidence drops below threshold
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale

claim: Production incident playbook must define detect-diagnose-contain-fix-prevent cycle with ITSM integration
actionable: Build incident response: detect via eval dashboard → diagnose via tracing → contain by versioning prompts/deflecting → fix using eval data → prevent by adding test cases
source_title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale
